metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_228
metadata.comment:
Rationale: this paper demonstrates that GRIPAP1(GRASP1) binds Rab4-GTP and is predominantly associated with Rab4-positive endosomes using EM and immunofluorescence co-localization of endogenous GRASP1 and Rab4, and live-imaging showed that GRASP1 moved together with mobile Rab4 endosomes in dendrites of hippocampal neurons (figure 3). These data together indicate that GRASP1 is localized to postsynaptic early endosome membrane by binding to Rab4 (extrinsic).

29/6/2017 Pim
- There is synaptic context; "GRASP-1 is associated with punctate structures that occasionally extend beyond the dendritic shaft (arrowheads in Figure 3C), overlap with the synaptic markers PSD-95 (arrowheads in Figure 3D) and Bassoon (arrowheads in Figure 3E).. Experimental description: from M&M; "Primary hippocampal cultures were prepared from embryonic day 18 (E18) rat brains"

"Anti-GRASP-1 (#5285) was generated by immunizing rabbits with GST-GRASP-1(1-378) and used for immuno electronmicroscopy."

"The rat GRASP-1 (accession NM_053807) smartpool siRNA (cat# L-096315-01) was from Dharmacon".
NCBI GenBank accession NM_053807 confirms GRASP-1 streetname refers to gene GRIPAP1.

"GRASP-1 truncation constructs and GRASP-1 mutant lacking aa280-300 were made with PCR from full-length GRASP-1 cDNA 1 [28]. GRASP-1* rescue constructs were prepared by a PCR-based strategy to introduce four silent substitutions in the target site. The target sequence GCTCTCTGAGAAATTGAAA was modified into GCTTTCGGAAAAGTTGAAA."

Nucleotide BLAST of this sequence confirmed this is gene GRIPAP1.

checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
